competitors.name: Keycloak
competitors.pricing: Free / Open Source (managed hosting from $0–$1,999/mo via Phase Two)
competitors.keyDifference: Java-based with significant operational complexity; DedgeAuth offers simpler deployment with built-in magic-link, Windows SSO/AD, and multi-tenant branding
competitors.notes: Most established open-source IAM. OIDC, OAuth 2.0, SAML 2.0, MFA, SSO, social login, passkeys, LDAP/AD federation, RBAC. Red Hat backed. Apache 2.0 license

competitors.name: Authentik
competitors.keyDifference: Flow-based authentication model is flexible but complex; DedgeAuth provides opinionated defaults (JWT + magic-link + Windows SSO) for enterprise Windows environments
competitors.notes: Modern open-source IdP with polished UI. OAuth 2.0, SAML, LDAP, SCIM, proxy/forward auth. Visual flow editor for custom login flows

competitors.name: Ory (Kratos/Hydra)
competitors.keyDifference: Highly modular requiring assembly of multiple services; DedgeAuth is a single unified service combining auth, JWT, magic-link, AD integration, and multi-tenant branding
competitors.notes: Modular open-source IAM: Kratos (users), Hydra (OAuth 2.0/OIDC), Oathkeeper (proxy), Keto (authorization). Pick components as needed

competitors.name: ZITADEL
competitors.keyDifference: Cloud-first with self-hosting as secondary; DedgeAuth is self-hosted-first with Windows AD/SSO as a core feature
competitors.notes: API-first identity platform. Hosted login, modern auth, SSO, social logins, RBAC, custom workflows. Native multi-tenancy support

competitors.name: Kotauth
competitors.keyDifference: Newer and less battle-tested; DedgeAuth includes Windows SSO/AD and magic-link as first-class features vs standard OAuth flows
competitors.notes: Docker-native self-hosted auth. OAuth 2.0, OIDC, multi-tenancy, TOTP, social OAuth, webhooks, per-tenant theming. Single Docker command deploy

competitors.name: Auth0 (Okta)
competitors.keyDifference: Cloud-only SaaS with per-user pricing that scales expensively; DedgeAuth is fully self-hosted with no per-user fees and native Windows AD/SSO
competitors.notes: Industry-standard managed auth. Universal login, social connections, MFA, passwordless, M2M auth. Acquired by Okta. Extensive SDK ecosystem
